name: H200
benchNote: ESTIMATED conservative defaults. 70B-class from NVIDIA NIM benchmarking docs (Llama-3.3-70B FP8 on 2x H200, 2,338 output tok/s total, halved and rounded down). 8B-class from the same docs (Llama-3.1-8B FP8 on 1x H200, 12,826 tok/s, rounded down). Serving/batched mode, not single stream. Override with a measured value. NOTE: this FP8 figure is conservative; the RTX PRO 6000 default uses an FP4 source. Defaults use different precisions and are NOT a head-to-head ranking; enter measured values for comparisons.
notes: Larger private inference, long context, high memory GPU serving. SXM (HGX baseboards) or NVL PCIe variants.

name: RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
benchNote: ESTIMATED conservative defaults. 70B-class from NVIDIA TensorRT-LLM perf-overview (Llama-3.3-70B NVFP4, 1,724 output tok/s per GPU, rounded down). 8B-class from a measured vLLM FP16 run (3,196 tok/s, unquantized; FP8/FP4 serves faster). Override with a measured value. NOTE: FP4 source; not directly comparable to the H200 FP8 default.
notes: Practical private inference: departmental agents, RAG, embeddings, small and medium open models. PCIe Gen5, MIG up to 4 instances.

name: Instinct MI355X
benchNote: ESTIMATED conservative defaults. 70B-class from MLPerf Inference v6.0 AMD submission (Llama2-70B FP4 server scenario, 12,535 tok/s on 8x MI355X, divided per GPU and rounded down). 8B-class from AMD ROCm blog (Llama-3.1-8B FP8 vLLM, 9,979 tok/s, rounded down). Override with a measured value.
notes: High memory private inference and large model workloads. Required warning: validate ROCm, model serving engine, and application stack compatibility before recommending.

name: ProLiant Compute DL380a Gen12
gpuSupport: Up to 16x NVIDIA L4, or up to 10x L40S / H100 NVL / H200 NVL, or up to 8x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition
notes: Departmental AI, enterprise inference, private agent pilots, RAG and agent serving, medium private inference.

name: ProLiant Compute XD685
gpuSupport: Eight NVIDIA B300 / B200 / H200, or eight AMD Instinct MI355X accelerators
notes: Large private inference, AI platform scale, training and tuning, larger accelerators. Accelerator support claims from the HPE product listing; verify exact configuration with HPE before recommending.
